title: Filter the Rows
description: Welcome to Rank Challenge. Marketing only wants the accounts that are currently active. Extend the query so it returns `id` and `name` from `users` but only the rows where the `status` column equals `'active'`.
objectives: Match the status column against the active value
hints: WHERE goes after the FROM clause.
String literals are wrapped in single quotes: 'active'.

files.content:
-- TODO: only keep the rows whose account state is active
SELECT id, name FROM users;

parseRules.failMessage: Add a WHERE clause to filter rows.
